figures.caption: Figure 1: Vanilla Agent saturates on head entities and stops early, while SPADER keeps expanding toward long-tail launch sites under diversity-aware incentives.
Figure 6: ReAct: early stop after one search round leads to severe under-coverage.
Figure 9: broad-intent ambiguity: a plausible but misaligned interpretation leads to low overlap with the target set.
Table 4: Full ablation results of SPADER on Qwen3-8B. “w/o” denotes removing one component from SPADER.
Figure 4: Cumulative distinct entities per interaction turn on QAMPARI.
Figure 3: Search efficiency comparison on the Qwen38B backbone. The y-axis represents the average search calls needed to yield one valid entity.

Figure 2: Overview of the SPADER framework. After sampling parallel trajectories, the framework evaluates each Search and Answer action using a Diversity-Aware Exploration Reward. Subsequently, Step-wise Peer Advantage (SPA) aligns these trajectories by decision steps to compute future returns (Gt) and step-wise advantages, which jointly optimize the policy through step-wise GRPO.
Figure 8: SPADER without information gain: search actions drift into repetitive, low-gain calls with degraded stopping behavior.
Figure 5: The standard system prompt template.
Figure 7: SPADER: incremental exploration expands coverage step by step.
